Clarity.justification: The paper is well-structured with clear sections, mechanistic derivations (e.g., outer-product decomposition ∇_W ℓ_i = (p_i - y_i) ⊗ h(x_i)), tables, and figures. Ideas are articulated logically, though minor repetition around unexecuted Stage 2 and post-hoc criterion adjustments slightly reduces coherence.

Novelty.justification: Applies per-sample gradient norms (equivalent to single-checkpoint EL2N) as a continuous minority proxy in spurious correlation settings with a mechanistic account via BatchNorm equalization. Builds incrementally on DFR, JTT, and EL2N without major new paradigms or benchmarks.

Soundness.justification: Methods and code are consistent (reproduces AUC=0.914, ratio=8.805 at epoch 5 on Waterbirds with h_norm_std_ratio≈0.10). However, major flaws include unexecuted Stage 2 (no WGA results), single seed, one dataset, and post-hoc replacement of balance_deviation criterion after failure (0.379 vs ≤0.10).

Significance.justification: Addresses the important problem of label-free robustness to spurious correlations. Proxy signal quality is demonstrated, but lack of worst-group accuracy results or downstream impact limits contributions and lasting field influence.

strengths: Clear mechanistic derivation and efficient forward-hook implementation
Strong proxy metrics (AUC 0.914, 8.8x ratio) with feature-norm verification
weaknesses: Full pipeline (Stage 2 last-layer retraining) unexecuted with no WGA results
Post-hoc criterion redesign after gate failure and single-seed/single-dataset scope
